citations.description: Recall dedup via norm_id — only for prior concerns, not within-verdict dedup
partition_findings: no filtering of empty/contentless findings
Search for 'contentless', 'de-duplicate', 'B2 B4', 'no checklist_item', 'suppress empty', 'duplicate finding' across all of src/ and tests/ returned no matches

findings.criterion: Finding discovery de-duplicates findings and suppresses contentless findings (empty body / no `checklist_item`); a fixture reproduces B2/B4.
findings.detail: No implementation was found that de-duplicates findings within a single verdict at the finding discovery stage or suppresses contentless findings (empty body / no `checklist_item`). The `pass1.py` recall mechanism deduplicates against prior concerns via `norm_id` (lines 506-511), but this does not address within-verdict duplicate surfacing or contentless-finding suppression. `partition_findings` in `orchestrator.py` does not filter empty/contentless findings. No test fixture reproducing B2 (contentless finding with no body and no checklist_item) or B4 (duplicate findings for the same claim) was found anywhere in the test suite. Searches for 'contentless', 'de-duplicate', 'B2', 'B4', 'empty body', 'no checklist_item' across both src and tests returned no relevant results.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
